action.reasoning: Observation: Latest inspect_render.py validated exact renderer call model.renderer(model.decoder, triplane, rays_o, rays_d) producing [1,256,256,3] RGB tensor; predict.py test on 20 val objects succeeded (all Completed rendering prints, correct RGBA saves, no crash). Full test eval crashed midway (~after 40 objs, around uid starting 4645) with exit 1 despite many successes; prior best LPIPS=0.6021 used fallback orbit on errors. Analysis: Custom rays with rel_az=(target_az - input_az)%360 + exact per-target elev/dist + robust up-vector/eps fully addresses root cause of poor LPIPS (default spherical orbit az starts at ~0 mismatching dataset's ~185° avg relative az + varying elev). Crash likely from rare numerical edge cases (elev near ±90°, NaNs in rays for specific test cameras). Zero-shot TripoSR with pose-aligned renderer remains optimal (exact task match, <1B params, no training needed, fits all constraints/budget). Decision: Fully update predict.py (complete non-truncated code from validated inspect + per-view try/except with model.render fallback + blank image last-resort + tensor-to-RGBA handling for all shapes/keys + per-obj gc/cache + directions.clone() for safety); test on full val (expect ~5-7min based on prior timings) to confirm no crashes before submit_eval for major LPIPS improvement.
